question_text: Which of the following best explains why a swimming pool appears shallower than it actually is?

options.label: A
options.text: Because light is absorbed by the water

options.label: B
options.text: Because light refracts as it leaves the water, bending away from the normal

options.label: C
options.text: Because light is reflected at the water surface

options.label: D
options.text: Because light travels faster in water than in air

correct_answer: Because light refracts as it leaves the water, bending away from the normal
explanation: When light travels from water (a denser medium) to air (a less dense medium), it bends away from the normal line. This refraction makes objects underwater appear closer to the surface than they actually are, making the pool look shallower than it really is.
